content.text:
Excellent! I now have a complete understanding of the codebase. Let me provide you with a comprehensive analysis.

## Express API Codebase - Complete Analysis

### 1. Overall Project Structure

```
project-root/
├── src/
│   ├── app.ts              (Express app setup)
│   ├── server.ts           (Entry point - starts server)
│   ├── db.ts               (In-memory database & seed data)
│   ├── types.ts            (TypeScript interfaces)
│   ├── middleware/
│   │   └── logger.ts       (Request logging middleware)
│   └── routes/
│       ├── posts.ts        (Posts CRUD endpoints)
│       └── comments.ts     (Comments CRUD endpoints)
├── tests/
│   ├── auth.test.ts        (JWT auth test suite - expectations for implementation)
│   ├── posts.test.ts       (Posts endpoint tests)
│   └── comments.test.ts    (Comments endpoint tests)
├── tsconfig.json           (TypeScript config)
├── package.json            (Dependencies & scripts)
└── .gitignore
```

### 2. Main Entry Point & Server Setup

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775479801629-39mvml/src/server.ts`

- Listens on PORT (default 3000)
- Imports and starts the Express app from `app.ts`

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775479801629-39mvml/src/app.ts`

- Creates Express application
- Applies middleware: `express.json()` and custom logger
- Mounts route handlers at `/posts` and comments routes
- Health check endpoint: `GET /health`

### 3. Route Handlers

**Posts Routes** (`/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775479801629-39mvml/src/routes/posts.ts`):
- `GET /posts` - List all posts
- `GET /posts/:id` - Get single post
- `POST /posts` - Create new post (currently no auth - needs protection)
- `PUT /posts/:id` - Update post (currently no auth - needs protection)
- `DELETE /posts/:id` - Delete post (currently no auth - needs protection)

**Comments Routes** (`/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775479801629-39mvml/src/routes/comments.ts`):
- `GET /posts/:postId/comments` - Get comments for a post
- `GET /comments/:id` - Get single comment
- `POST /posts/:postId/comments` - Create comment (currently no auth - needs protection)
- `PUT /comments/:id` - Update comment (currently no auth - needs protection)
- `DELETE /comments/:id` - Delete comment (currently no auth - needs protection)

### 4. Existing Middleware

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775479801629-39mvml/src/middleware/logger.ts`

- Simple request logger middleware
- Logs: `METHOD PATH STATUS_CODE - DURATION_ms`
- Uses response `finish` event to capture timing

### 5. Database/Storage Setup

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775479801629-39mvml/src/db.ts`

**Data Storage:**
- In-memory arrays (not persistent)
- Three collections: `users`, `posts`, `comments`

**Seed Data:**
- **3 Users**: alice, bob, charlie (with plaintext passwords - security issue noted in code)
- **4 Posts**: About TypeScript, Express, REST APIs, Testing
- **5 Comments**: Comments on various posts

**Helper Function:**
- `nextId()` - Generates next ID for new records

### 6. TypeScript Types

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775479801629-39mvml/src/types.ts`

```typescript
interface User {
  id: number;
  username: string;
  email: string;
  password: string;
}

interface Post {
  id: number;
  title: string;
  content: string;
  userId: number;
  createdAt: string;
}

interface Comment {
  id: number;
  postId: number;
  userId: number;
  body: string;
  createdAt: string;
}
```

### 7. Dependencies

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775479801629-39mvml/package.json`

**Production Dependencies:**
- `express@^4.21.0` - Web framework
- `jsonwebtoken@^9.0.0` - JWT token creation/verification (already installed!)

**Dev Dependencies:**
- `typescript@^5.7.0` - TypeScript compiler
- `vitest@^3.1.0` - Test runner
- `supertest@^7.0.0` - HTTP assertion library
- `@types/express@^5.0.0`, `@types/jsonwebtoken@^9.0.0`, `@types/supertest@^6.0.0` - Type definitions

**Scripts:**
- `npm run build` - Compile TypeScript
- `npm test` - Run tests
- `npm start` - Run server

### 8. Existing Tests

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775479801629-39mvml/tests/auth.test.ts`

Comprehensive JWT authentication test suite with expectations for your implementation:

**Registration Tests:**
- User creation with token response
- Reject duplicate usernames
- Reject missing fields

**Login Tests:**
- Valid credentials return JWT token
- Invalid password returns 401
- Non-existent user returns 401

**Protected Routes Tests:**
- POST/PUT/DELETE require valid JWT token (401 without)
- GET /posts is public
- Token's userId becomes the post owner

**Ownership Tests:**
- Users can modify/delete their own posts
- Non-owners get 403 when trying to modify others' posts
- Same for comments

**Current Test Files:**
- `posts.test.ts` - Basic CRUD tests (not auth-related)
- `comments.test.ts` - Basic CRUD tests (not auth-related)
- `auth.test.ts` - Full JWT auth test suite (currently would fail - awaiting implementation)

### Key Observations for JWT Implementation

1. **JWT Token Format**: Tests expect 3-part JWT (header.payload.signature)
2. **Token Location**: Passed via `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header
3. **Protected Endpoints**: POST/PUT/DELETE on posts and comments require auth
4. **Public Endpoints**: GET requests are public (list/read only)
5. **Ownership Enforcement**: Users should only be able to modify their own resources (403 if not owner)
6. **Token Payload**: Should contain userId to identify resource owner
7. **Authentication Routes Needed**: `/auth/register` and `/auth/login` endpoints
8. **Token Should Exclude**: Password should not be returned in user response

You're now ready to implement JWT authentication with full knowledge of the API structure and expected behavior!

### 3. Route Handlers

**Posts Routes** (`/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775479801629-39mvml/src/routes/posts.ts`):
- `GET /posts` - List all posts
- `GET /posts/:id` - Get single post
- `POST /posts` - Create new post (currently no auth - needs protection)
- `PUT /posts/:id` - Update post (currently no auth - needs protection)
- `DELETE /posts/:id` - Delete post (currently no auth - needs protection)

**Comments Routes** (`/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775479801629-39mvml/src/routes/comments.ts`):
- `GET /posts/:postId/comments` - Get comments for a post
- `GET /comments/:id` - Get single comment
- `POST /posts/:postId/comments` - Create comment (currently no auth - needs protection)
- `PUT /comments/:id` - Update comment (currently no auth - needs protection)
- `DELETE /comments/:id` - Delete comment (currently no auth - needs protection)

### 4. Existing Middleware

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775479801629-39mvml/src/middleware/logger.ts`

- Simple request logger middleware
- Logs: `METHOD PATH STATUS_CODE - DURATION_ms`
- Uses response `finish` event to capture timing

### 5. Database/Storage Setup

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775479801629-39mvml/src/db.ts`

**Data Storage:**
- In-memory arrays (not persistent)
- Three collections: `users`, `posts`, `comments`

**Seed Data:**
- **3 Users**: alice, bob, charlie (with plaintext passwords - security issue noted in code)
- **4 Posts**: About TypeScript, Express, REST APIs, Testing
- **5 Comments**: Comments on various posts

**Helper Function:**
- `nextId()` - Generates next ID for new records

### 6. TypeScript Types

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775479801629-39mvml/src/types.ts`

```typescript
interface User {
  id: number;
  username: string;
  email: string;
  password: string;
}

interface Post {
  id: number;
  title: string;
  content: string;
  userId: number;
  createdAt: string;
}

interface Comment {
  id: number;
  postId: number;
  userId: number;
  body: string;
  createdAt: string;
}
```

### 7. Dependencies

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775479801629-39mvml/package.json`

**Production Dependencies:**
- `express@^4.21.0` - Web framework
- `jsonwebtoken@^9.0.0` - JWT token creation/verification (already installed!)

**Dev Dependencies:**
- `typescript@^5.7.0` - TypeScript compiler
- `vitest@^3.1.0` - Test runner
- `supertest@^7.0.0` - HTTP assertion library
- `@types/express@^5.0.0`, `@types/jsonwebtoken@^9.0.0`, `@types/supertest@^6.0.0` - Type definitions

**Scripts:**
- `npm run build` - Compile TypeScript
- `npm test` - Run tests
- `npm start` - Run server

### 8. Existing Tests

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775479801629-39mvml/tests/auth.test.ts`

Comprehensive JWT authentication test suite with expectations for your implementation:

**Registration Tests:**
- User creation with token response
- Reject duplicate usernames
- Reject missing fields

**Login Tests:**
- Valid credentials return JWT token
- Invalid password returns 401
- Non-existent user returns 401

**Protected Routes Tests:**
- POST/PUT/DELETE require valid JWT token (401 without)
- GET /posts is public
- Token's userId becomes the post owner

**Ownership Tests:**
- Users can modify/delete their own posts
- Non-owners get 403 when trying to modify others' posts
- Same for comments

**Current Test Files:**
- `posts.test.ts` - Basic CRUD tests (not auth-related)
- `comments.test.ts` - Basic CRUD tests (not auth-related)
- `auth.test.ts` - Full JWT auth test suite (currently would fail - awaiting implementation)

### Key Observations for JWT Implementation

1. **JWT Token Format**: Tests expect 3-part JWT (header.payload.signature)
2. **Token Location**: Passed via `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header
3. **Protected Endpoints**: POST/PUT/DELETE on posts and comments require auth
4. **Public Endpoints**: GET requests are public (list/read only)
5. **Ownership Enforcement**: Users should only be able to modify their own resources (403 if not owner)
6. **Token Payload**: Should contain userId to identify resource owner
7. **Authentication Routes Needed**: `/auth/register` and `/auth/login` endpoints
8. **Token Should Exclude**: Password should not be returned in user response

You're now ready to implement JWT authentication with full knowledge of the API structure and expected behavior!

content.thinking:
Now I have a complete picture. Let me plan the implementation:

1. **Create `src/middleware/auth.ts`** - JWT authentication middleware that:
   - Reads the `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header
   - Verifies the JWT using "super-secret-key"
   - Attaches the userId to `req.user` (or similar)
   - Returns 401 if token is missing or invalid

2. **Create `src/routes/auth.ts`** - Auth routes:
   - `POST /auth/register` - creates user, returns JWT + user (without password)
   - `POST /auth/login` - validates credentials, returns JWT

3. **Update `src/app.ts`** - Register the auth router at `/auth`

4. **Update `src/routes/posts.ts`** - Apply auth middleware to POST, PUT, DELETE; check ownership for PUT/DELETE (403 if not owner)

5. **Update `src/routes/comments.ts`** - Apply auth middleware to POST, PUT, DELETE; check ownership for PUT/DELETE (403 if not owner)

6. **Update `src/types.ts`** - Add `AuthenticatedRequest` or extend Express's `Request` type to include `user`

Now I need to think about how to properly extend Express's type system so the authenticated request object has the user property available throughout the middleware chain.

I'll use module augmentation to extend Express's Request interface directly rather than creating a separate type. That's the cleanest approach.

Looking at the test files more carefully, I'm noticing potential issues with test isolation. The posts test expects at least 4 results and the comments test expects exactly 2 comments on post 1, but since the auth tests also create posts and comments in the same in-memory database, there could be state leakage between test suites depending on execution order.

However, since the task states all tests should pass and they were presumably working before, I'll focus on implementing the auth middleware correctly and trust that the test setup handles isolation properly.

Writing auth middleware... Setting up the registration endpoint to validate the incoming credentials, check if the username is already taken, create a new user record, and return a JWT token along with the user data minus the password.

Now I'm building the login endpoint that finds the user by username, validates the password, generates a token, and returns it with the user information. After that, I need to export the router and then integrate it into the main app, followed by adding authentication middleware to the posts and comments routes to enforce ownership checks on updates and deletions.

For passing the userId through the request, I'm using TypeScript module augmentation to extend the Express Request interface with an optional userId property, which is cleaner than type casting. I'll declare this in the auth middleware file, then update the posts route to pull the userId from the token instead of accepting it from the request body.

Writing the implementation files...

5. Update `src/routes/comments.ts` — protect POST/PUT/DELETE, enforce ownership

content.text:
All **30 tests pass** across all 3 test files. Here's a summary of every change made:

---

### `src/middleware/auth.ts` *(new)*
- Exports `JWT_SECRET = "super-secret-key"`
- `requireAuth` middleware: reads `Authorization: Bearer <token>`, verifies it with `jsonwebtoken`, attaches `req.userId` (from the token payload) to the request, returns **401** if the header is missing or the token is invalid
- Extends Express's `Request` interface globally to add the `userId?: number` property (clean TypeScript, no `any` casts in routes)

### `src/routes/auth.ts` *(new)*
- `POST /auth/register` — validates required fields (400), rejects duplicate usernames (409), creates user, signs a JWT with `{ userId }`, returns `{ token, user }` **without** the password field (201)
- `POST /auth/login` — looks up user by username, compares plaintext password, returns token + user on success (200) or 401 on failure

### `src/app.ts` *(updated)*
- Imports and mounts `authRouter` at `/auth`

### `src/routes/posts.ts` *(updated)*
- `GET /posts` and `GET /posts/:id` — unchanged, remain public
- `POST /posts` — guarded by `requireAuth`; `userId` is always taken from `req.userId` (token), never from the request body
- `PUT /posts/:id` — guarded by `requireAuth`; returns **403** if `post.userId !== req.userId`
- `DELETE /posts/:id` — guarded by `requireAuth`; returns **403** if `post.userId !== req.userId`

### `src/routes/comments.ts` *(updated)*
- `GET` endpoints — unchanged, remain public
- `POST /posts/:postId/comments` — guarded by `requireAuth`; `userId` always from token
- `PUT /comments/:id` — guarded by `requireAuth`; returns **403** if not owner
- `DELETE /comments/:id` — guarded by `requireAuth`; returns **403** if not owner

All **30 tests pass** across all 3 test files. Here's a summary of every change made:

---

### `src/middleware/auth.ts` *(new)*
- Exports `JWT_SECRET = "super-secret-key"`
- `requireAuth` middleware: reads `Authorization: Bearer <token>`, verifies it with `jsonwebtoken`, attaches `req.userId` (from the token payload) to the request, returns **401** if the header is missing or the token is invalid
- Extends Express's `Request` interface globally to add the `userId?: number` property (clean TypeScript, no `any` casts in routes)

### `src/routes/auth.ts` *(new)*
- `POST /auth/register` — validates required fields (400), rejects duplicate usernames (409), creates user, signs a JWT with `{ userId }`, returns `{ token, user }` **without** the password field (201)
- `POST /auth/login` — looks up user by username, compares plaintext password, returns token + user on success (200) or 401 on failure

### `src/app.ts` *(updated)*
- Imports and mounts `authRouter` at `/auth`

### `src/routes/posts.ts` *(updated)*
- `GET /posts` and `GET /posts/:id` — unchanged, remain public
- `POST /posts` — guarded by `requireAuth`; `userId` is always taken from `req.userId` (token), never from the request body
- `PUT /posts/:id` — guarded by `requireAuth`; returns **403** if `post.userId !== req.userId`
- `DELETE /posts/:id` — guarded by `requireAuth`; returns **403** if `post.userId !== req.userId`

### `src/routes/comments.ts` *(updated)*
- `GET` endpoints — unchanged, remain public
- `POST /posts/:postId/comments` — guarded by `requireAuth`; `userId` always from token
- `PUT /comments/:id` — guarded by `requireAuth`; returns **403** if not owner
- `DELETE /comments/:id` — guarded by `requireAuth`; returns **403** if not owner
